content:
You're managing a nine-figure company, raising children, and trying to keep everyone healthy. But what if I told you that managing your family's wellness could be as systematic and effective as running your business? That's exactly what Julie E discovered after 40 years in functional and energy medicine, and her results speak for themselves: she raised a son who has never missed a single day of school due to illness.

In this transformative episode of The Manage Her, host Aimee Rickabus sits down with Julie E to unpack her revolutionary three-step framework that puts you back in control of your family's health without the overwhelm or constant worry that keeps so many moms up at night.

The Foundation: Understanding Your Biofield

Julie's approach starts with something most of us have never heard of but encounter every single day: your biofield. Think of it as an invisible energy field around your body that, when intact, creates the foundation for unlimited healing. But here's the problem that's affecting every single one of us right now - our phones are systematically destroying this field.

Your smartphone isn't just a convenience tool; it's actively working against your health. The electromagnetic frequencies (EMF) emitted by our devices create chaos in our biofield, leading to false symptoms and making it nearly impossible to identify true root causes of health issues. But before you panic about going off-grid, Julie has a surprisingly simple solution: a Q-disc sticker that can actually transform your phone from a health destroyer into a healing tool.

This isn't about fear-mongering or asking you to give up technology. It's about understanding that as leaders, we need to make informed decisions about the tools we use daily. When your biofield is protected and intact, your body can finally show you what's really going on instead of giving you mixed signals.

Step Two: Your Body's Assembly Line

Julie revolutionizes how we think about detox by comparing our eleven detox organs to an assembly line. Just like in your business, when one part of the system gets clogged, everything downstream suffers. Your liver, kidneys, lymphatic system, skin, lungs, and other detox organs work together in a coordinated effort, and understanding this system gives you the power to support it effectively.

One of the most eye-opening revelations from this conversation is how our children's bodies communicate their needs. If your child is constantly getting bruises on their shins or seems unusually clumsy, it's not just an active kid being a kid - it's often a sign that their kidneys need support, which affects their equilibrium and balance.

Julie shares a powerful story about giving her toddler an enema to break a fever in just fifteen minutes. Before you dismiss this as too alternative, understand the science: fevers in children often indicate toxins backing up in the intestines. When you clear the gut, you clear the fever. This is about understanding your child's body as a system and responding appropriately rather than just masking symptoms.

The Sleep Rule That Changes Everything

If you're struggling with perimenopause, hormone imbalances, or just feeling like you're running on empty, Julie's five-hour head-on-pillow rule might be the game-changer you've been looking for. This isn't about getting five hours of sleep - it's about having five consecutive hours where your head maintains contact with your pillow.

During these uninterrupted hours, your glands rejuvenate and regulate all your hormones. Aimee shares how implementing this one rule transformed her own sleep and energy levels. It's not about perfection; it's about consistency and understanding that your body has specific requirements for optimal function.

When you wake up at 3 AM, 4 AM, or 5 AM, you're not just waking up to use the bathroom. Your body is telling you that specific detox organs are running out of resources at those exact times. Understanding your body's circadian rhythm gives you the information you need to support the right systems at the right times.

Hidden Blocks: Scars, Trauma, and Dental Work

The third step in Julie's framework addresses something most people never consider: how physical scars and dental work create energetic traffic jams in your body. That C-section scar isn't just cosmetic - it can be the root cause of back, neck, and shoulder pain years later because it disrupts the energy meridians running through your body.

Julie reveals the surprising connection between dental infections and breast cancer, showing how our bodies are interconnected systems where problems in one area can manifest as symptoms somewhere completely different. This isn't about creating fear around medical procedures; it's about understanding that healing often requires addressing these hidden blocks that conventional medicine doesn't consider.

Taking Back Control

What makes Julie's approach so powerful is that it puts you back in the driver's seat. You're already managing complex systems in your business and your home. Your family's health can be managed with the same systematic, informed approach you bring to everything else in your life.

This conversation is permission to stop outsourcing your family's health decisions and start managing wellness like the leader you are. Julie provides practical tools like mud therapy that can pull out fifty percent of toxins in one application, and a sixty-second nervous system reset technique you can implement immediately.

You don't have to choose between being a high-achieving leader and having a healthy family. With the right framework and understanding, you can excel at both. Julie's three-step approach gives you the roadmap to transform how you think about and manage your family's wellness, turning it from a source of stress into an area where you can lead with confidence and achieve remarkable results.

quiz.title: What's Your Family Wellness Leadership Style?
quiz.description: Discover how you naturally approach your family's health and wellbeing as the leader of your household. Find out which strategies from this episode will transform your approach.

questions.question: When your child complains of feeling 'off' but has no obvious symptoms, what's your first instinct?

options.text: I immediately sense something deeper is going on and start observing their energy and behavior patterns
I start mentally running through potential causes and consider what tests or assessments might reveal the issue
I make sure they're getting enough sleep, water, and good food while keeping an eye on how they're feeling
I wait to see if specific symptoms develop so I know exactly what I'm dealing with

questions.question: How do you typically approach trying new wellness strategies for your family?

options.text: I'm drawn to approaches that address the whole person and trust what feels right for our family's unique needs
I need to understand the science behind it and see evidence of results before implementing anything new
I prefer starting with small, simple changes that won't disrupt our established routines too much
I usually try new things when our current approach isn't working and we need a solution

questions.question: When you're managing your own health while caring for your family, you tend to:

options.text: Pay attention to how your energy and mood affect the whole family's wellbeing
Look for efficient systems that optimize both your health and your family management time
Focus on sustainable habits that you can maintain consistently without burning out
Put your family's immediate needs first and address your own health when you have time

questions.question: Your ideal approach to preventing illness in your family would be:

options.text: Understanding the energetic and emotional factors that contribute to physical symptoms
Having a comprehensive prevention protocol based on the latest research and testing
Maintaining strong foundational practices like good sleep, nutrition, and stress management
Knowing exactly what to do when early symptoms appear so you can act quickly

questions.question: When you hear about something like 'biofield disruption' from technology, your reaction is:

options.text: That makes total sense - I've noticed our family feels different around too much screen time
I want to understand the mechanism and see studies showing how this impacts health outcomes
I'm interested but want to know about simple, practical solutions that won't overwhelm our lifestyle
I'm curious if this could explain some of the unexplained symptoms we've been dealing with

questions.question: Your biggest challenge in managing your family's wellness is:

options.text: Trusting my instincts when others dismiss my concerns or when I can't find practitioners who understand my approach
Filtering through conflicting information to find evidence-based approaches that actually work
Finding time and energy for wellness practices without adding more stress to our already full lives
Being proactive instead of reactive - I want to prevent problems before they become urgent

results.title: The Intuitive Healer
results.description: You have a natural instinct for sensing when something's off with your family's health, and you trust your gut feelings about what they need. You're drawn to understanding the deeper connections between symptoms and root causes. Your intuitive approach is a gift, but sometimes you second-guess yourself when facing complex health challenges. This episode will give you the scientific framework to back up your instincts and help you understand how your family's biofield affects the signals you're picking up on.

results.title: The Research-Driven Optimizer
results.description: You approach your family's wellness like a strategic project, diving deep into research and wanting to understand the 'why' behind every recommendation. You love having systems and frameworks that make sense logically. Your thorough approach ensures nothing gets overlooked, but sometimes you can get stuck in analysis paralysis. This episode is perfect for you because Julie's systematic three-step framework will give you the clear, evidence-based structure you crave while helping you take confident action.

results.title: The Gentle Steadiness
results.description: You prefer consistent, simple routines that don't disrupt your family's flow, and you're naturally cautious about trying new approaches to health and wellness. You value stability and gradual changes over dramatic overhauls. Your steady approach creates a calm foundation for your family, but you might miss opportunities for breakthrough improvements. This episode will show you gentle, foundational changes that feel manageable while creating profound shifts in your family's wellbeing.

results.title: The Responsive Firefighter
results.description: You're excellent at handling health crises when they arise and can quickly mobilize to get your family the care they need in urgent situations. You're practical and action-oriented when problems present themselves. Your responsive nature means your family knows they can count on you when things go wrong, but you'd love to get ahead of issues before they become emergencies. This episode will teach you how to read your family's early warning signals and create preventive strategies that reduce those midnight ER visits.
